cases.title: Sears v. Nashville Presbytery
precedent_refs: SJC 2020-07 TE Wilbourne v. Pacific Presbytery
SJC 2019-10 TE Evans v. Arizona Presbytery
cases.synopsis: TE Ian Sears appealed his deposition by Nashville Presbytery, which arose from a BCO 38-1 case without process after he signed a confession of guilt regarding two incidents of sexual sin in 2010-2011. The SJC unanimously denied all five specifications of error and affirmed the Presbytery's judgment and censure of deposition.

cases.title: Martinez v. Pacific Presbytery
precedent_refs: 2023-10 Complaint of REs Martinez and Cook v. Pacific
cases.synopsis: RE Martinez complained that Pacific Presbytery erred by sustaining a complaint against the Valley Presbyterian Church Session's decision to close worship with benedictions rather than doxologies. The SJC sustained the complaint 19-1, holding that the Session had constitutional authority under BCO 12-5.e to make this liturgical decision and that it was not a clear error of judgment or discretion.
